The `updateAuthorizer` method must be called to update the API's authorizer to include the newly created API key.
The `importApiKeys` method must be called to import all newly created API keys into the current stage of the API.
The `createUsagePlanKey` method must be called to associate the newly created API key with the correct usage plan.
pt: O `createDeployment` method deve be called so o API can be reimplantared para include o newly criard API key.
O `updateAuthorizer` method deve be called para update o API's authorizer para include o newly criard API key.
O `importApiKeys` method deve be called para import all newly criard API keys into o current stage of o API.
O `createUsagePlanKey` method deve be called para associate o newly criard API key com o correct usage plan.

correct.D: Correct. API Gateway API keys do not grant access by creation alone. After `CreateApiKey`, the key must be associated with a usage plan by `createUsagePlanKey`; otherwise the request can receive `403 Forbidden`. Concept assessed: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Exam trap: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.

wrong.A: Incorrect. Redeploying publishes API configuration but does not attach a key to a usage plan. Concept assessed: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Exam trap: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Authorizers validate identities; API key usage plans are separate. Concept assessed: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Exam trap: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Importing keys is not the same as associating an existing key to a plan. Concept assessed: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Exam trap: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.

correct.D: Correta. API keys do API Gateway não concedem acesso apenas por serem criadas. Depois de `CreateApiKey`, a chave precisa ser associada a um usage plan com `createUsagePlanKey`; caso contrário, a requisição pode receber `403 Forbidden`. Conceito avaliado: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Armadilha da prova: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Reimplantar publica configuração da API, mas não associa a chave a um usage plan. Conceito avaliado: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Armadilha da prova: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Authorizers validam identidades; usage plans de API key são separados. Conceito avaliado: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Armadilha da prova: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Importar chaves não é o mesmo que associar uma chave existente a um plano. Conceito avaliado: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Armadilha da prova: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.

question.en: A Developer is writing a mobile application that allows users to view images from an S3 bucket. The users must be able to log in with their Amazon login, as well as Facebook and/or Google accounts. How can the Developer provide this authentication functionality?
question.pt: Um Developer is writing a mobile application that permitirs usuários para view images de an S3 bucket. O usuários deve be able para log em com their Amazon login, como well como Facebook and/or Google accounts. Como o Developer pode provide this authentication funçãoality?

en: Use Amazon Cognito with web identity federation.
Use Amazon Cognito with SAML-based identity federation.
Use AWS IAM Access/Secret keys in the application code to allow `Get*` on the S3 bucket.
Use AWS STS `AssumeRole` in the application code and assume a role with `Get*` permissions on the S3 bucket.
pt: Use Amazon Cognito com web identity federation.
Use Amazon Cognito com SAML-based identity federation.
Use AWS IAM Access/Secret keys em o application código para permitir `Get*` em o S3 bucket.
Use AWS STS `AssumeRole` em o application código e assume a role com `Get*` permissions em o S3 bucket.

correct.A: Correct. Amazon Cognito supports web identity federation with social providers such as Login with Amazon, Facebook, and Google, and can issue temporary AWS credentials for S3 access. Concept assessed: Cognito federation. Exam trap: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.

wrong.B: Incorrect. SAML targets enterprise federation, not Amazon/Facebook/Google social login. Concept assessed: Cognito federation. Exam trap: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Embedding long-lived AWS keys in app code is insecure. Concept assessed: Cognito federation. Exam trap: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.
wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (Cognito federation). The correct mechanism is required because amazon Cognito supports web identity federation with social providers such as Login with Amazon, Facebook, and Google, and can issue temporary AWS credentials for S3 access. Concept assessed: Cognito federation. Exam trap: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.

correct.A: Correta. Amazon Cognito suporta web identity federation com provedores sociais como Login with Amazon, Facebook e Google, e pode emitir credenciais temporárias AWS para acesso ao S3. Conceito avaliado: Cognito federation. Armadilha da prova: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.

wrong.B: Incorreta. SAML é para federação corporativa, não login social Amazon/Facebook/Google. Conceito avaliado: Cognito federation. Armadilha da prova: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Embutir chaves AWS de longa duração no código é inseguro. Conceito avaliado: Cognito federation. Armadilha da prova: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (Cognito federation). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque amazon Cognito suporta web identity federation com provedores sociais como Login with Amazon, Facebook e Google, e pode emitir credenciais temporárias AWS para acesso ao S3. Conceito avaliado: Cognito federation. Armadilha da prova: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.

question.en: A Developer wants access to make the log data of an application running on an EC2 instance available to systems administrators. Which of the following enables monitoring of this metric in Amazon CloudWatch?
question.pt: Um Developer wants acessar para make o log dados of an application executada em an EC2 instância available para systems administrators. Quais das seguintes opções habilitars monitoring of this métrica em Amazon CloudWatch?

en: Retrieve the log data from CloudWatch using the `GetMetricData` API call.
Retrieve the log data from AWS CloudTrail using the `LookupEvents` API call.
Launch a new EC2 instance, configure Amazon CloudWatch Events, and then install the application.
Install the Amazon CloudWatch Logs agent on the EC2 instance that the application is running on.
pt: Retrieve o log dados de CloudWatch usando o `GetMetricData` API call.
Retrieve o log dados de AWS CloudTrail usando o `LookupEvents` API call.
Launch a new EC2 instância, configurar Amazon CloudWatch Events, e then install o application.
Install o Amazon CloudWatch Logs agent em o EC2 instância that o application is executada em.

correct.D: Correct. EC2 application logs are not collected automatically. The CloudWatch Logs agent ships application log files to CloudWatch Logs for search, monitoring, metrics, and alarms. Concept assessed: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Exam trap: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (CloudWatch Logs on EC2). The correct mechanism is required because eC2 application logs are not collected automatically. The CloudWatch Logs agent ships application log files to CloudWatch Logs for search, monitoring, metrics, and alarms. Concept assessed: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Exam trap: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.
wrong.B: Incorrect. CloudTrail audits AWS API calls, not application log files. Concept assessed: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Exam trap: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.
wrong.C: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (CloudWatch Logs on EC2). The correct mechanism is required because eC2 application logs are not collected automatically. The CloudWatch Logs agent ships application log files to CloudWatch Logs for search, monitoring, metrics, and alarms. Concept assessed: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Exam trap: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.

correct.D: Correta. Logs de aplicação em EC2 não são coletados automaticamente. O CloudWatch Logs agent envia os logs para CloudWatch Logs para busca, monitoramento, métricas e alarmes. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Armadilha da prova: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (CloudWatch Logs on EC2). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque logs de aplicação em EC2 não são coletados automaticamente. O CloudWatch Logs agent envia os logs para CloudWatch Logs para busca, monitoramento, métricas e alarmes. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Armadilha da prova: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.
wrong.B: Incorreta. CloudTrail audita APIs AWS, não arquivos de log de aplicação. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Armadilha da prova: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (CloudWatch Logs on EC2). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque logs de aplicação em EC2 não são coletados automaticamente. O CloudWatch Logs agent envia os logs para CloudWatch Logs para busca, monitoramento, métricas e alarmes. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Armadilha da prova: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.

question.en: A nightly batch job loads 1 million new records into a DynamoDB table. The records are only needed for one hour, and the table needs to be empty by the next night's batch job. Which is the MOST efficient and cost-effective method to provide an empty table?
question.pt: A nightly batch job loads 1 million new records into a DynamoDB tabela. O records are only needed para one hour, e o tabela precisa be empty por o next night's batch job. Which is o MOST eficiente e econômica method para provide an empty tabela?

en: Use `DeleteItem` using a `ConditionExpression`.
Use `BatchWriteItem` to empty all of the rows.
Write a recursive function that scans and calls out `DeleteItem`.
Create and then delete the table after the task has completed.
pt: Use `DeleteItem` usando a `ConditionExpression`.
Use `BatchWriteItem` para empty all of o rows.
Write a recursive função that scans e calls out `DeleteItem`.
Create e then delete o tabela after o task has completed.

correct.D: Correct. Deleting and recreating the DynamoDB table avoids scanning and deleting one million items, which would consume write capacity and many API calls. Concept assessed: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Exam trap: deleting every item individually.

wrong.A: Incorrect. Item-by-item deletion consumes capacity and API calls. Concept assessed: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Exam trap: deleting every item individually.
wrong.B: Incorrect. BatchWriteItem still deletes item by item and consumes write capacity. Concept assessed: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Exam trap: deleting every item individually.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Item-by-item deletion consumes capacity and API calls. Concept assessed: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Exam trap: deleting every item individually.

correct.D: Correta. Excluir e recriar a tabela DynamoDB evita varrer e excluir um milhão de itens, o que consumiria write capacity e muitas chamadas de API. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Armadilha da prova: deleting every item individually.

question.en: A company has an application that logs all information to Amazon S3. Whenever there is a new log file, an AWS Lambda function is invoked to process the log files. The code works, gathering all of the necessary information. However, when checking the Lambda function logs, duplicate entries with the same request ID are found. What is causing the duplicate entries?

O código works, gathering all of o necessary information. However, when checking o Lambda função logs, duplicate entries com o same request ID are found. What is causando o duplicate entries?

en: The S3 bucket name was specified incorrectly.
The Lambda function failed, and the Lambda service retired the invocation with a delay.
There was an S3 outage, which caused duplicate entries of the sale log file.
The application stopped intermittently and then resumed.

O Lambda função failed, e o Lambda service retired o invocation com a delay.

A aplicação stopped intermittently e then resumed.

correct.B: Correct. S3-to-Lambda asynchronous invocations are at-least-once. If the function fails or times out, Lambda can retry the same event and create duplicate processing. Concept assessed: Lambda retry behavior. Exam trap: assuming exactly-once event processing.

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (Lambda retry behavior). The correct mechanism is required because s3-to-Lambda asynchronous invocations are at-least-once. If the function fails or times out, Lambda can retry the same event and create duplicate processing. Concept assessed: Lambda retry behavior. Exam trap: assuming exactly-once event processing.

question.en: A company uses Amazon DynamoDB for managing and tracking orders. The DynamoDB table is partitioned based on the order date. According to AWS best practices, how can this issue be resolved with MINIMAL costs?

According para AWS best practices, how can this issue be resolved com MINIMAL costs?

en: Create a new DynamoDB table for every order date.
Increase the read and write capacity units of the DynamoDB table.
Add a random number suffix to the partition key values.
Add a global secondary index to the DynamoDB table.
pt: Create a new DynamoDB tabela para every order date.
Increase o ler e gravar capacity units of o DynamoDB tabela.
Add a random number suffix para o partition key values.
Add a global secondary index para o DynamoDB tabela.

correct.C: Correct. Partitioning by date creates a hot partition during sales spikes. Adding a random suffix spreads writes across partition key values with minimal cost. Concept assessed: DynamoDB hot partitions. Exam trap: buying capacity when key design is the issue.

question.en: A Development team currently supports an application that uses an in-memory store to save accumulated game results. Individual results are stored in a database. Where should the team store these accumulated game results to BEST allow for consistent results without impacting performance?

en: Amazon S3.
Amazon RDS.
Amazon ElastiCache.
Amazon Kinesis.
pt: Amazon S3.
Amazon RDS.
Amazon ElastiCache.
Amazon Kinesis.

correct.C: Correct. ElastiCache is a shared low-latency in-memory store, avoiding inconsistent per-instance memory while preserving performance under Auto Scaling. Concept assessed: shared in-memory state. Exam trap: keeping accumulated state in local memory.

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (shared in-memory state). The correct mechanism is required because elastiCache is a shared low-latency in-memory store, avoiding inconsistent per-instance memory while preserving performance under Auto Scaling. Concept assessed: shared in-memory state. Exam trap: keeping accumulated state in local memory.
wrong.B: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: shared in-memory state. Exam trap: keeping accumulated state in local memory.
wrong.D: Incorrect. Kinesis streams records; it is not a shared state/cache store. Concept assessed: shared in-memory state. Exam trap: keeping accumulated state in local memory.

correct.C: Correta. ElastiCache é um armazenamento em memória compartilhado e de baixa latência, evitando memória local inconsistente e mantendo performance com Auto Scaling. Conceito avaliado: shared in-memory state. Armadilha da prova: keeping accumulated state in local memory.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (shared in-memory state). Conceito avaliado: shared in-memory state. Armadilha da prova: keeping accumulated state in local memory.
wrong.B: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: shared in-memory state. Armadilha da prova: keeping accumulated state in local memory.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Kinesis transmite registros; não é cache/estado compartilhado. Conceito avaliado: shared in-memory state. Armadilha da prova: keeping accumulated state in local memory.

question.en: A company maintains an application responsible for processing several thousand external callbacks each day. The company's System administrators want to know how many callbacks are being received on a rolling basis, and they want this data available for 10 days. The company also wants the ability to issue automated alerts if the number of callbacks exceeds the defined thresholds. What is the MOST cost-effective way to address the need to track and alert on these statistics?
question.pt: Uma empresa maintains an application responsible para processing several thousand external callbacks each day. A empresa's System administrators want para know how many callbacks are being received em a rolling basis, e they want this dados available para 10 days. A empresa also wants o ability para issue automated alerts if o number of callbacks exceeds o defined thresholds. Qual é a forma MAIS econômica way para address o precisam track e alert em these statistics?

en: Push callback data to an Amazon RDS database that can be queried to show historical data and to alert on exceeded thresholds.
Push callback data to AWS X-Ray and use AWS Lambda to query, display, and alert on exceeded thresholds.
Push callback data to Amazon Kinesis Data Streams and invoke an AWS Lambda function that stores data in Amazon DynamoDB and sends the required alerts.
Push callback data to Amazon CloudWatch as a custom metric and use the CloudWatch alerting mechanisms to alert System Administrators.
pt: Push callback dados para an Amazon RDS dadosbase that can be queried para show historical dados e para alert em exceeded thresholds.
Push callback dados para AWS X-Ray e use AWS Lambda para query, display, e alert em exceeded thresholds.
Push callback dados para Amazon Kinesis Data Streams e invoke an AWS Lambda função that armazenars dados em Amazon DynamoDB e sends o required alerts.
Push callback dados para Amazon CloudWatch como a customizado métrica e use o CloudWatch alerting mechanisms para alert System Administrators.

correct.D: Correct. CloudWatch custom metrics are built for numeric time-series, retention, dashboards, and alarms, making callback count tracking and alerting cost-effective. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: building databases/streams for simple counters.

wrong.A: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: building databases/streams for simple counters.
wrong.B: Incorrect. X-Ray traces requests; it does not front APIs. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: building databases/streams for simple counters.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Kinesis streams records; it is not a shared state/cache store. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: building databases/streams for simple counters.

correct.D: Correta. CloudWatch custom metrics são feitas para séries temporais numéricas, retenção, dashboards e alarmes, tornando contagem e alerta de callbacks econômicos. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch custom metrics. Armadilha da prova: building databases/streams for simple counters.

wrong.A: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch custom metrics. Armadilha da prova: building databases/streams for simple counters.
wrong.B: Incorreta. X-Ray rastreia requisições; não expõe APIs. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch custom metrics. Armadilha da prova: building databases/streams for simple counters.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Kinesis transmite registros; não é cache/estado compartilhado. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch custom metrics. Armadilha da prova: building databases/streams for simple counters.

question.en: A company has a website that is developed in PHP and WordPress and is launched using AWS Elastic Beanstalk. There is a new version of the website that needs to be deployed in the Elastic Beanstalk environment. The company cannot tolerate having the website offline if an update fails. Deployments must have minimal impact and rollback as soon as possible. What deployment method should be used?

There is a new version of o website that precisa be implantared em o Elastic Beanstalk environment. A empresa cannot tolerate having o website offline if an update fails. Deployments deve have minimal impact e rollback como soon como possible. What implantarment method should be used?

en: All at once.
Rolling.
Snapshots.
Immutable.
pt: All at once.
Rolling.
Snapshots.
Immutabela.

correct.D: Correct. Immutable deployment launches a separate set of instances and shifts traffic only after health checks pass, enabling fast rollback with minimal impact. Concept assessed: Elastic Beanstalk deployment policies. Exam trap: using rolling/all-at-once when failure cannot affect users.

wrong.A: Incorrect. All-at-once can cause downtime if the update fails. Concept assessed: Elastic Beanstalk deployment policies. Exam trap: using rolling/all-at-once when failure cannot affect users.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Rolling changes existing capacity and rollback is less isolated than immutable. Concept assessed: Elastic Beanstalk deployment policies. Exam trap: using rolling/all-at-once when failure cannot affect users.
wrong.C: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (Elastic Beanstalk deployment policies). question.en: A company has a multi-tiered web application on AWS. During a recent spike in traffic, one of the primary relational databases on Amazon RDS could not serve all the traffic. Some read queries for repeatedly accessed items failed, so users received error messages. What can be done to minimize the impact on database read queries MOST efficiently during future traffic spikes?

Some ler queries para repeatedly acessared items failed, so usuários received error mensagens. What can be done para minimize o impact em dadosbase ler queries MOST eficientely during future traffic spikes?

en: Use Amazon S3 to cache database query results.
Use Amazon RDS as a custom origin for Amazon CloudFront.
Use local storage and memory on Amazon EC2 instances to cache data.
Use Amazon ElastiCache in front of the primary database to cache data.

Use Amazon RDS como a customizado origin para Amazon CloudFront.
Use local storage e memory em Amazon EC2 instâncias para cache dados.

Read: 6 read capacity. `unitsWrite`: 70 write capacity units.

question.en: A Developer is creating an AWS Lambda function to process a stream of data from an Amazon Kinesis Data Stream. When the Lambda function parses the data and encounters a missing field, it exits the function with an error. The function is generating duplicate records from the Kinesis stream. When the Developer looks at the stream output without the Lambda function, there are no duplicate records. What is the reason for the duplicates?
question.pt: Um Developer is creating an AWS Lambda função para process a stream of dados de an Amazon Kinesis Data Stream. When o Lambda função parses o dados e encounters a missing field, it exits o função com an error. O função is generating duplicate records de o Kinesis stream. When o Developer looks at o stream output without o Lambda função, there are no duplicate records. What is o reason para o duplicates?

en: The Lambda function did not advance the Kinesis stream pointer to the next record after the error.
The Lambda event source used asynchronous invocation, resulting in duplicate records.
The Lambda function did not handle the error, and the Lambda service attempted to reprocess the data.
The Lambda function is not keeping up with the amount of data coming from the stream.
pt: O Lambda função did not advance o Kinesis stream pointer para o next record after o error.
O Lambda event source used asynchronous invocation, resulting em duplicate records.
O Lambda função did not handle o error, e o Lambda service attempted para reprocess o dados.
O Lambda função is not keeping up com o amount of dados coming de o stream.

correct.C: Correct. For Kinesis event sources, Lambda retries failed batches. If parsing errors are not handled, the same records can be reprocessed and appear duplicated. Concept assessed: Lambda stream retries. Exam trap: blaming Kinesis for retry duplicates.

question.en: A company is developing an application that will run on several Amazon EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group and can access a database running on Amazon EC2. question.en: An AWS Lambda function must read data from an Amazon RDS MySQL database in a VPC and also reach a public endpoint over the internet to get additional data. Which steps must be taken to allow the function to access both the RDS resource and the public endpoint? (Select TWO)
question.pt: Uma função AWS Lambda precisa ler dados de um Amazon RDS MySQL em uma VPC e também acessar um endpoint público na internet. Quais etapas permitem acesso ao RDS e ao endpoint público? (Selecione DUAS)

en: Modify the default configuration for the Lambda function to associate it with an Amazon VPC private subnet.
Modify the default network access control list to allow outbound traffic.
Add a NAT Gateway to the VPC.
Modify the default configuration of the Lambda function to associate it with a VPC public subnet.
Add an environmental variable to the Lambda function to allow outbound internet access.

en: Remove the origin from the CloudFront configuration and add it again.
Disable forwarding of query strings and request headers from the CloudFront distribution configuration.

Disable the CloudFront distribution and enable it again to update all the edge locations.

Invalidate all o application objetos de o edge caches.

question.en: A Developer is writing an imaging micro service on AWS Lambda. The service is dependent on several libraries that are not available in the Lambda runtime environment. Which strategy should the Developer follow to create the Lambda deployment package?

en: Create a `ZIP` file with the source code and all dependent libraries.
Create a `ZIP` file with the source code and a script that installs the dependent libraries at runtime.
Create a `ZIP` file with the source code. Stage the dependent libraries on an Amazon S3 bucket indicated by the Lambda environment variable `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`.
Create a `ZIP` file with the source code and a buildspec.yaml file that installs the dependent libraries on AWS Lambda.

Create a `ZIP` arquivo com o source código e a buildspec.yaml arquivo that installs o dependent libraries em AWS Lambda.

question.en: A Developer is designing a fault-tolerant environment where client sessions will be saved. How can the Developer ensure that no sessions are lost if an Amazon EC2 instance fails?
question.pt: Um Developer is designing a fault-tolerant environment where client sessions will be saved. Como o Developer pode ensure that no sessions are lost if an Amazon EC2 instância fails?

en: Use sticky sessions with an Elastic Load Balancer target group.
Use Amazon SQS to save session data.
Use Amazon DynamoDB to perform scalable session handling.
Use Elastic Load Balancer connection draining to stop sending requests to failing instances.
pt: Use sticky sessions com an Elastic Load Balancer target group.
Use Amazon SQS para save session dados.
Use Amazon DynamoDB para perform escalável session handling.
Use Elastic Load Balancer connection draining para stop sending requests para failing instâncias.

correct.C: Correct. DynamoDB is a durable shared session store; any EC2 instance can retrieve the session after another instance fails. Concept assessed: external session state. Exam trap: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.

wrong.A: Incorrect. Sticky sessions still lose data if the instance fails. Concept assessed: external session state. Exam trap: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.
wrong.B: Incorrect. SQS queues messages; it does not manage REST APIs. Concept assessed: external session state. Exam trap: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.
wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (external session state). The correct mechanism is required because dynamoDB is a durable shared session store; any EC2 instance can retrieve the session after another instance fails. Concept assessed: external session state. Exam trap: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.

correct.C: Correta. DynamoDB é armazenamento de sessão compartilhado e durável; qualquer EC2 recupera a sessão se outra falhar. Conceito avaliado: external session state. Armadilha da prova: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Sticky sessions ainda perdem dados se a instância falhar. Conceito avaliado: external session state. Armadilha da prova: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.

Armadilha da prova: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.

question.en: In a move toward using microservices, a company's Management team has asked all Development teams to build their services so that API requests depend only on that service's data store. One team is building a Payments service which has its own database; the service needs data that originates in the Accounts database. Both are using Amazon DynamoDB. What approach will result in the simplest, decoupled, and reliable method to get near-real time updates from the Accounts database?

One team is building a Payments service which has its own dadosbase; o service needs dados that originates em o Accounts dadosbase. Both are usando Amazon DynamoDB. What approach will result em o simplest, decoupled, e reliable method para get near-real time updates de o Accounts dadosbase?

en: Use Amazon Glue to perform frequent ETL updates from the Accounts database to the Payments database.
Use Amazon ElastiCache in Payments, with the cache updated by triggers in the Accounts database.
Use Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose to deliver all changes from the Accounts database to the Payments database.
Use Amazon DynamoDB Streams to deliver all changes from the Accounts database to the Payments database.
pt: Use Amazon Glue para perform frequent ETL updates de o Accounts dadosbase para o Payments dadosbase.
Use Amazon ElastiCache em Payments, com o cache updated por triggers em o Accounts dadosbase.
Use Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose para deliver all changes de o Accounts dadosbase para o Payments dadosbase.
Use Amazon DynamoDB Streams para deliver all changes de o Accounts dadosbase para o Payments dadosbase.

correct.D: Correct. DynamoDB Streams captures item-level changes and can feed downstream updates to another service database in near real time. Concept assessed: DynamoDB Streams. Exam trap: batch ETL or cache for change propagation.

correct.D: Correta. DynamoDB Streams captura mudanças por item e alimenta atualizações downstream em outro banco quase em tempo real. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB Streams. Armadilha da prova: batch ETL or cache for change propagation.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Glue é ETL batch, não propagação quase real-time. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB Streams. Armadilha da prova: batch ETL or cache for change propagation.

question.en: A Developer is writing a serverless application that requires that an AWS Lambda function be invoked every 10 minutes. What is an automated and serverless way to trigger the function?

question.en: A Developer is creating a mobile application that will not require users to log in. What is the MOST efficient method to grant users access to AWS resources?

Create credentials using AWS KMS and apply these credentials to users when using the application.

question.en: A company has multiple Developers located across the globe who are updating code incrementally for a development project. When Developers upload code concurrently, internet connectivity is slow and it is taking a long time to upload code for deployment in AWS Elastic Beanstalk. Which step will result in minimized upload and deployment time with the LEAST amount of administrative effort?

When Developers upload código concurrently, internet connectivity is slow e it is taking a long time para upload código para implantarment em AWS Elastic Beanstalk. Qual etapa will result em minimized upload e implantarment time com o LEAST amount of administrative effort?

en: Allow the Developers to upload the code to an Amazon S3 bucket, and deploy it directly to Elastic Beanstalk.
Allow the Developers to upload the code to a central FTP server to deploy the application to Elastic Beanstalk.
Create an AWS CodeCommit repository, allow the Developers to commit code to it, and then directly deploy the code to Elastic Beanstalk.
Create a code repository on an Amazon EC2 instance so that all Developers can update the code, and deploy the application from the instance to Elastic Beanstalk.
pt: Allow o Developers para upload o código para an Amazon S3 bucket, e implantar it directly para Elastic Beanstalk.
Allow o Developers para upload o código para a central FTP server para implantar o application para Elastic Beanstalk.
Create an AWS CodeCommit repository, permitir o Developers para commit código para it, e then directly implantar o código para Elastic Beanstalk.
Create a código repository em an Amazon EC2 instância so that all Developers can update o código, e implantar o application de o instância para Elastic Beanstalk.

correct.C: Correct. CodeCommit lets distributed developers push incremental Git changes to a managed repository and deploy from there to Elastic Beanstalk. Concept assessed: CodeCommit deployment source. Exam trap: using FTP/EC2/S3 full uploads.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Beanstalk não implanta Swagger serverless. Conceito avaliado: CodeCommit deployment source. Armadilha da prova: using FTP/EC2/S3 full uploads.

question.en: An organization is storing large files in Amazon S3, and is writing a web application to display meta-data about the files to end-users. Based on the metadata a user selects an object to download. The organization needs a mechanism to index the files and provide single-digit millisecond latency retrieval for the metadata. What AWS service should be used to accomplish this?
question.pt: An organization is storing large arquivos em Amazon S3, e is writing a web application para display meta-dados about o arquivos para end-usuários. Based em o metadados a user selects an objeto para download. O organization needs a mechanism para index o arquivos e provide single-digit millisecond latência retrieval para o metadados. What AWS service should be used para accomplish this?

en: Amazon DynamoDB.
Amazon EC2.
AWS Lambda.
Amazon RDS.
pt: Amazon DynamoDB.
Amazon EC2.
AWS Lambda.
Amazon RDS.

correct.A: Correct. DynamoDB provides indexed metadata lookup with single-digit millisecond latency while S3 stores the large files. Concept assessed: DynamoDB metadata index. Exam trap: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.

wrong.B: Incorrect. EC2 adds server management and is unnecessary for this managed/serverless requirement. Concept assessed: DynamoDB metadata index. Exam trap: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Lambda runs code but is not an API management facade. Concept assessed: DynamoDB metadata index. Exam trap: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.
wrong.D: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: DynamoDB metadata index. Exam trap: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.

correct.A: Correta. DynamoDB fornece consulta indexada de metadados com milissegundos de um dígito enquanto S3 armazena arquivos grandes. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB metadata index. Armadilha da prova: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.

wrong.B: Incorreta. EC2 adiciona servidores e é desnecessário para esse requisito gerenciado/serverless. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB metadata index. Armadilha da prova: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Lambda executa código, mas não é fachada de gerenciamento de API. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB metadata index. Armadilha da prova: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.
wrong.D: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB metadata index. Armadilha da prova: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.

question.en: A stock market monitoring application uses Amazon Kinesis for data ingestion. During simulated tests of peak data rates, the Kinesis stream cannot keep up with the incoming data. What step will allow Kinesis to accommodate the traffic during peak hours?
question.pt: A stock market monitoring application usa Amazon Kinesis para dados ingestion. During simulated tests of peak dados rates, o Kinesis stream cannot keep up com o incoming dados. What step will permitir Kinesis para accommodate o traffic during peak hours?

en: Install the Kinesis Producer Library (KPL) for ingesting data into the stream.
Reduce the data retention period to allow for more data ingestion using `DecreaseStreamRetentionPeriod`.
Increase the shard count of the stream using `UpdateShardCount`.
Ingest multiple records into the stream in a single call using `PutRecords`.

Ingest multiple records into o stream em a single call usando `PutRecords`.

correct.C: Correct. Kinesis Data Streams throughput scales by shard count; `UpdateShardCount` increases capacity for peak ingestion. Concept assessed: Kinesis shards. Exam trap: optimizing producer calls when shards are saturated.

wrong.D: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: Kinesis shards. Armadilha da prova: optimizing producer calls when shards are saturated.

question.en: A company caches session information for a web application in an Amazon DynamoDB table. The company wants an automated way to delete old items from the table. What is the simplest way to do this?

Add an attribute with the expiration time; enable the `Time To Live` feature based on that attribute.

Add an attribute with the expiration time; name the attribute `ItemExpiration`.

question.en: An application is expected to process many files. Each file takes four minutes to process each AWS Lambda invocation. The Lambda function does not return any important data. What is the fastest way to process all the files?

en: First split the files to make them smaller, then process with synchronous RequestResponse Lambda invocations.
Make synchronous RequestResponse Lambda invocations and process the files one by one.
Make asynchronous Event Lambda invocations and process the files in parallel.
First join all the files, then process it all at once with an asynchronous Event Lambda invocation.
pt: First split o arquivos para make them smaller, then process com synchronous RequestResponse Lambda invocations.
Make synchronous RequestResponse Lambda invocations e process o arquivos one por one.
Make asynchronous Event Lambda invocations e process o arquivos em parallel.
First join all o arquivos, then process it all at once com an asynchronous Event Lambda invocation.

correct.C: Correct. Asynchronous Event invocations let independent Lambda executions process files in parallel without waiting for return data. Concept assessed: Lambda async parallelism. Exam trap: serial synchronous processing.

question.en: The upload of a 15 GB object to Amazon S3 fails. The error message reads: `Your proposed upload exceeds the maximum allowed object size.`. What technique will allow the Developer to upload this object?

question.en: A company needs a new REST API that can return information about the contents of an Amazon S3 bucket, such as a count of the objects stored in it. The company has decided that the new API should be written as a microservice using AWS Lambda and Amazon API Gateway. How should the Developer ensure that the microservice has the necessary access to the Amazon S3 bucket, while adhering to security best practices?
question.pt: Uma empresa needs a new REST API that can return information about o contents of an Amazon S3 bucket, such como a count of o objetos armazenados em it. A empresa has decided that o new API should be written como a microservice usando AWS Lambda e Amazon API Gateway. Como o Developer ensure that o microservice has o necessary acessar para o Amazon S3 bucket, while adhering para security best practices?

en: Create an IAM user that has permissions to access the Amazon S3 bucket, and store the IAM user credentials in the Lambda function source code.
Create an IAM role that has permissions to access the Amazon S3 bucket and assign it to the Lambda function as its execution role.
Create an Amazon S3 bucket policy that specifies the Lambda service as its principal and assign it to the Amazon S3 bucket.
Create an IAM role, attach the AmazonS3FullAccess managed policy to it, and assign the role to the Lambda function as its execution role.
pt: Create an IAM user that has permissions para acessar o Amazon S3 bucket, e armazenar o IAM user credenciais em o Lambda função source código.
Create an IAM role that has permissions para acessar o Amazon S3 bucket e assign it para o Lambda função como its execution role.
Create an Amazon S3 bucket policy that specifies o Lambda service como its principal e assign it para o Amazon S3 bucket.
Create an IAM role, attach o AmazonS3FullAccess gerenciado policy para it, e assign o role para o Lambda função como its execution role.

correct.B: Correct. A Lambda execution role with least-privilege S3 permissions provides temporary credentials without storing IAM user keys in code. Concept assessed: Lambda execution role. Exam trap: embedded keys or overbroad access.

wrong.A: Incorrect. Creating IAM users for app users does not scale and is unsafe. Concept assessed: Lambda execution role. Exam trap: embedded keys or overbroad access.
wrong.C: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (Lambda execution role). The correct mechanism is required because a Lambda execution role with least-privilege S3 permissions provides temporary credentials without storing IAM user keys in code. Concept assessed: Lambda execution role. Exam trap: embedded keys or overbroad access.
wrong.D: Incorrect. FullAccess is broader than least privilege. Concept assessed: Lambda execution role. Exam trap: embedded keys or overbroad access.

correct.B: Correta. Uma execution role da Lambda com permissões S3 mínimas fornece credenciais temporárias sem armazenar chaves no código. Conceito avaliado: Lambda execution role. Armadilha da prova: embedded keys or overbroad access.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Criar IAM users para usuários da app não escala e é inseguro. Conceito avaliado: Lambda execution role. Armadilha da prova: embedded keys or overbroad access.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (Lambda execution role). Conceito avaliado: Lambda execution role. Armadilha da prova: embedded keys or overbroad access.
wrong.D: Incorreta. FullAccess é mais amplo que least privilege. Conceito avaliado: Lambda execution role. Armadilha da prova: embedded keys or overbroad access.

question.en: An organization is using Amazon CloudFront to ensure that its users experience low-latency access to its web application. The organization has identified a need to encrypt all traffic between users and CloudFront, and all traffic between CloudFront and the web application. How can these requirements be met? (Choose TWO)
question.pt: An organization usa Amazon CloudFront para ensure that its usuários experience low-latência acessar para its web application. O organization has identified a precisam encrypt all traffic between usuários e CloudFront, e all traffic between CloudFront e o web application. How can these requirements be met? (Escolha DUAS)

en: Use AWS KMS to encrypt traffic between CloudFront and the web application.
Set the Origin Protocol Policy to `HTTPS Only`.
Set the Origin's HTTP Port to `443`.
Set the Viewer Protocol Policy to `HTTPS Only` or `Redirect HTTP to HTTPS`.
Enable the CloudFront option `Restrict Viewer Access`.
pt: Use AWS KMS para encrypt traffic between CloudFront e o web application.
Set o Origin Protocol Policy para `HTTPS Only`.
Set o Origin's HTTP Port para `443`.
Set o Viewer Protocol Policy para `HTTPS Only` ou `Redirect HTTP to HTTPS`.
Enable o CloudFront option `Restrict Viewer Access`.

correct.B: Correct. Viewer Protocol Policy enforces HTTPS from users to CloudFront; Origin Protocol Policy HTTPS Only enforces HTTPS from CloudFront to the origin. Concept assessed: CloudFront TLS. Exam trap: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption.
correct.D: Correct. Viewer Protocol Policy enforces HTTPS from users to CloudFront; Origin Protocol Policy HTTPS Only enforces HTTPS from CloudFront to the origin. Concept assessed: CloudFront TLS. Exam trap: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption.

wrong.A: Incorrect. KMS manages keys, not users/password reset/application directories. Concept assessed: CloudFront TLS. Exam trap: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Changing the origin does not purge old edge cache. Concept assessed: CloudFront TLS. Exam trap: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption.
wrong.E: Incorrect. CloudFront caches HTTP content, not direct RDS query results or DynamoDB API reads. Concept assessed: CloudFront TLS. Exam trap: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption.

correct.B: Correta. Viewer Protocol Policy força HTTPS de usuários ao CloudFront; Origin Protocol Policy HTTPS Only força HTTPS do CloudFront à origem. Conceito avaliado: CloudFront TLS. Armadilha da prova: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption.
correct.D: Correta. Viewer Protocol Policy força HTTPS de usuários ao CloudFront; Origin Protocol Policy HTTPS Only força HTTPS do CloudFront à origem. Conceito avaliado: CloudFront TLS. Armadilha da prova: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption.

wrong.A: Incorreta. KMS gerencia chaves, não usuários/reset de senha. Conceito avaliado: CloudFront TLS. Armadilha da prova: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Mudar origem não limpa cache antigo. Conceito avaliado: CloudFront TLS. Armadilha da prova: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption.
wrong.E: Incorreta. CloudFront cacheia HTTP, não consultas RDS ou leituras DynamoDB diretas. Conceito avaliado: CloudFront TLS. Armadilha da prova: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption.

question.en: An application needs to use the IP address of the client in its processing. The application has been moved into AWS and has been placed behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB). However, all the client IP addresses now appear to be the same. The application must maintain the ability to scale horizontally. Based on this scenario, what is the MOST cost-effective solution to this problem?
question.pt: Uma aplicação precisa use o IP address of o client em its processing. A aplicação has been moved into AWS e has been placed behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB). However, all o client IP addresses now appear para be o same. A aplicação deve maintain o ability para scale horizontally. Based em this scenario, what is o MOST econômica solution para this problem?

en: Remove the application from the ALB. Delete the ALB and change Amazon Route 53 to direct traffic to the instance running the application.
Remove the application from the ALB. Create a Classic Load Balancer in its place. Direct traffic to the application using the HTTP protocol.
Alter the application code to inspect the `X-Forwarded-For` header. Ensure that the code can work properly if a list of IP addresses is passed in the header.
Alter the application code to inspect a custom header. Alter the client code to pass the IP address in the custom header.
pt: Remove o application de o ALB. Delete o ALB e change Amazon Route 53 para direct traffic para o instância running o application.
Remove o application de o ALB. Create a Classic Load Balancer em its place. Direct traffic para o application usando o HTTP protocol.
Alter o application código para inspect o `X-Forwarded-For` header. Ensure that o código can work properly if a list of IP addresses is passed em o header.
Alter o application código para inspect a customizado header. Alter o client código para pass o IP address em o customizado header.

correct.C: Correct. ALB preserves original client IP in `X-Forwarded-For`; reading that header keeps horizontal scaling and avoids replacing the load balancer. Concept assessed: ALB forwarded headers. Exam trap: removing ALB or custom client headers.

Concept assessed: ALB forwarded headers. Exam trap: removing ALB or custom client headers.
wrong.D: Incorrect. A custom header requires client changes; ALB already sets X-Forwarded-For. Concept assessed: ALB forwarded headers. Exam trap: removing ALB or custom client headers.

correct.C: Correta. ALB preserva o IP original no `X-Forwarded-For`; ler esse header mantém escalabilidade e evita trocar o load balancer. Conceito avaliado: ALB forwarded headers. Armadilha da prova: removing ALB or custom client headers.

wrong.B: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (ALB forwarded headers). question.en: The Developer for a retail company must integrate a fraud detection solution into the order processing solution. The fraud detection solution takes between ten and thirty minutes to verify an order. At peak, the web site can receive one hundred orders per minute. What is the most scalable method to add the fraud detection solution to the order processing pipeline?
question.pt: Uma solução de detecção de fraude leva de 10 a 30 minutos por pedido, e o site recebe até 100 pedidos por minuto. Qual é o método mais escalável para integrar a fraude ao pipeline de pedidos?

en: Add all new orders to an Amazon SQS queue. Configure a fleet of 10 EC2 instances spanning multiple AZs with the fraud detection solution installed on them to pull orders from this queue. Update the order with a pass or fails status.

Add all new orders to an SQS queue. Configure an Auto Scaling group that uses the queue depth metric as its unit of scale to launch a dynamically-sized fleet of EC2 instances spanning multiple AZs with the fraud detection solution installed on them to pull orders from this queue. Update the order with a pass or fails status.

Add all new orders to an Amazon Kinesis Stream. Subscribe a Lambda function to automatically read batches of records from the Kinesis Stream. The Lambda function includes the fraud detection software and will update the order with a pass or fail status.

Write all new orders to Amazon DynamoDB. Configure DynamoDB Streams to include all new orders. Subscribe a Lambda function to automatically read batches of records from the Kinesis Stream. The Lambda function includes the fraud detection software and will update the order with a pass or fail status.
pt: Add all new orders para an Amazon SQS fila. Configure a fleet of 10 EC2 instâncias spanning multiple AZs com o fraud detection solution installed em them para pull orders de this fila. Update o order com a pass ou fails status.

Configure an Auto Scaling group that usa o fila depth métrica como its unit of scale para launch a dynamically-sized fleet of EC2 instâncias spanning multiple AZs com o fraud detection solution installed em them para pull orders de this fila. Update o order com a pass ou fails status.

Add all new orders para an Amazon Kinesis Stream. Subscribe a Lambda função para automatically ler batches of records de o Kinesis Stream. O Lambda função includes o fraud detection software e will update o order com a pass ou fail status.

Write all new orders para Amazon DynamoDB. Configure DynamoDB Streams para include all new orders. Subscribe a Lambda função para automatically ler batches of records de o Kinesis Stream. O Lambda função includes o fraud detection software e will update o order com a pass ou fail status.

correct.B: Correct. SQS decouples order intake; Auto Scaling workers based on queue depth scale dynamically for long fraud checks across AZs. Concept assessed: queue-based scaling. Exam trap: fixed fleet or Lambda for 10-30 minute jobs.

Armadilha da prova: invented query/API credential approaches.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Query custom não invalida cache nativo. Conceito avaliado: API Gateway cache. Armadilha da prova: invented query/API credential approaches.

question.en: A Developer has been asked to build a real-time dashboard web application to visualize the key prefixes and storage size of objects in Amazon S3 buckets. Amazon DynamoDB will be used to store the Amazon S3 metadata. What is the optimal and MOST cost-effective design to ensure that the real-time dashboard is kept up to date with the state of the objects in the Amazon S3 buckets?
question.pt: Um Developer has been asked para build a real-time dashboard web application para visualize o key prefixes e storage size of objetos em Amazon S3 buckets. Amazon DynamoDB will be used para armazenar o Amazon S3 metadados. What is o optimal e MOST econômica design para ensure that o real-time dashboard is kept up para date com o state of o objetos em o Amazon S3 buckets?

en: Use an Amazon CloudWatch event backed by an AWS Lambda function. Issue an Amazon S3 API call to get a list of all Amazon S3 objects and persist the metadata within DynamoDB. Have the web application poll the DynamoDB table to reflect this change.
Use Amazon S3 Event Notification backed by a Lambda function to persist the metadata into DynamoDB. Have the web application poll the DynamoDB table to reflect this change.
Run a cron job within an Amazon EC2 instance to list all objects within Amazon S3 and persist the metadata into DynamoDB. Have the web application poll the DynamoDB table to reflect this change.
Create a new Amazon EMR cluster to get all the metadata about Amazon S3 objects; persist the metadata into DynamoDB. Have the web application poll the DynamoDB table to reflect this change.
pt: Use an Amazon CloudWatch event backed por an AWS Lambda função. Issue an Amazon S3 API call para get a list of all Amazon S3 objetos e persist o metadados within DynamoDB. Have o web application poll o DynamoDB tabela para reflect this change.
Use Amazon S3 Event Notification backed por a Lambda função para persist o metadados into DynamoDB. Have o web application poll o DynamoDB tabela para reflect this change.
Run a cron job within an Amazon EC2 instância para list all objetos within Amazon S3 e persist o metadados into DynamoDB. Have o web application poll o DynamoDB tabela para reflect this change.
Create a new Amazon EMR cluster para get all o metadados about Amazon S3 objetos; persist o metadados into DynamoDB. Have o web application poll o DynamoDB tabela para reflect this change.

correct.B: Correct. S3 Event Notifications trigger Lambda on object changes, letting Lambda update DynamoDB metadata incrementally and cheaply. Concept assessed: S3 event-driven metadata. Exam trap: polling/listing all objects.

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (S3 event-driven metadata). The correct mechanism is required because s3 Event Notifications trigger Lambda on object changes, letting Lambda update DynamoDB metadata incrementally and cheaply. Concept assessed: S3 event-driven metadata. Exam trap: polling/listing all objects.
wrong.C: Incorrect. EC2 adds server management and is unnecessary for this managed/serverless requirement. Concept assessed: S3 event-driven metadata. Exam trap: polling/listing all objects.
wrong.D: Incorrect. EMR is for big data processing, not caching repeated reads. Concept assessed: S3 event-driven metadata. Exam trap: polling/listing all objects.

correct.B: Correta. S3 Event Notifications acionam Lambda em mudanças de objeto, permitindo atualizar metadados no DynamoDB de forma incremental e barata. Conceito avaliado: S3 event-driven metadata. Armadilha da prova: polling/listing all objects.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (S3 event-driven metadata). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque s3 Event Notifications acionam Lambda em mudanças de objeto, permitindo atualizar metadados no DynamoDB de forma incremental e barata. Conceito avaliado: S3 event-driven metadata. Armadilha da prova: polling/listing all objects.
wrong.C: Incorreta. EC2 adiciona servidores e é desnecessário para esse requisito gerenciado/serverless. Conceito avaliado: S3 event-driven metadata. Armadilha da prova: polling/listing all objects.
wrong.D: Incorreta. EMR é big data, não cache de leituras. Conceito avaliado: S3 event-driven metadata. Armadilha da prova: polling/listing all objects.

question.en: A Developer must repeatedly and consistently deploy a serverless RESTful API on AWS. Which techniques will work? (Choose TWO)
question.pt: Um Developer deve implantar repetidamente e de forma consistente uma API RESTful serverless na AWS. Quais técnicas funcionam? (Escolha DUAS)

en: Define a Swagger file. Use AWS Elastic Beanstalk to deploy the Swagger file.
Define a Swagger file. Use AWS CodeDeploy to deploy the Swagger file.
Deploy a SAM template with an inline Swagger definition.
Define a Swagger file. Deploy a SAM template that references the Swagger file.
Define an inline Swagger definition in a Lambda function. Invoke the Lambda function.
pt: Define a Swagger arquivo. Use AWS Elastic Beanstalk para implantar o Swagger arquivo.
Define a Swagger arquivo. Use AWS CodeDeploy para implantar o Swagger arquivo.
Deploy a SAM template com an inline Swagger definition.
Define a Swagger arquivo. Deploy a SAM template that references o Swagger arquivo.
Define an inline Swagger definition em a Lambda função. Invoke o Lambda função.

correct.C: Correct. SAM supports serverless APIs with inline Swagger/OpenAPI or a referenced Swagger file for repeatable deployments. Concept assessed: SAM API definitions. Exam trap: deploying Swagger with Beanstalk/CodeDeploy.
correct.D: Correct. SAM supports serverless APIs with inline Swagger/OpenAPI or a referenced Swagger file for repeatable deployments. Concept assessed: SAM API definitions. Exam trap: deploying Swagger with Beanstalk/CodeDeploy.

wrong.A: Incorrect. Beanstalk does not deploy serverless API Swagger definitions. Concept assessed: SAM API definitions. Exam trap: deploying Swagger with Beanstalk/CodeDeploy.
wrong.B: Incorrect. CodeDeploy deploys revisions; it does not create APIs from Swagger. Concept assessed: SAM API definitions. Exam trap: deploying Swagger with Beanstalk/CodeDeploy.
wrong.E: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (SAM API definitions). The correct mechanism is required because sAM supports serverless APIs with inline Swagger/OpenAPI or a referenced Swagger file for repeatable deployments. Concept assessed: SAM API definitions. Exam trap: deploying Swagger with Beanstalk/CodeDeploy.

correct.C: Correta. SAM suporta APIs serverless com Swagger/OpenAPI inline ou arquivo Swagger referenciado para implantações repetíveis. Conceito avaliado: SAM API definitions. Armadilha da prova: deploying Swagger with Beanstalk/CodeDeploy.
correct.D: Correta. SAM suporta APIs serverless com Swagger/OpenAPI inline ou arquivo Swagger referenciado para implantações repetíveis. Conceito avaliado: SAM API definitions. Armadilha da prova: deploying Swagger with Beanstalk/CodeDeploy.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Beanstalk não implanta Swagger serverless. Conceito avaliado: SAM API definitions. Armadilha da prova: deploying Swagger with Beanstalk/CodeDeploy.
wrong.B: Incorreta. CodeDeploy implanta revisões; não cria API do Swagger. Conceito avaliado: SAM API definitions. Armadilha da prova: deploying Swagger with Beanstalk/CodeDeploy.
wrong.E: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (SAM API definitions). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque sAM suporta APIs serverless com Swagger/OpenAPI inline ou arquivo Swagger referenciado para implantações repetíveis. Conceito avaliado: SAM API definitions. Armadilha da prova: deploying Swagger with Beanstalk/CodeDeploy.

question.en: An existing serverless application processes uploaded image files. The process currently uses a single Lambda function that takes an image file, performs the processing, and stores the file in Amazon S3. Users of the application now require thumbnail generation of the images. Users want to avoid any impact to the time it takes to perform the image uploads. How can thumbnail generation be added to the application, meeting user requirements while minimizing changes to existing code?
question.pt: An existing serverless application processes uploaded image arquivos. O process currently usa a single Lambda função that takes an image arquivo, performs o processing, e armazenars o arquivo em Amazon S3. Usuários of o application now require thumbnail generation of o images. Usuários want para avoid any impact para o time it takes para perform o image uploads. How can thumbnail generation be added para o application, meeting user requirements while minimizing changes para existing código?

en: Change the existing Lambda function handling the uploads to create thumbnails at the time of upload. Have the function store both the image and thumbnail in Amazon S3.
Create a second Lambda function that handles thumbnail generation and storage. Change the existing Lambda function to invoke it asynchronously.
Create an S3 event notification with a Lambda function destination. Create a new Lambda function to generate and store thumbnails.
Create an S3 event notification to an SQS Queue. Create a scheduled Lambda function that processes the queue, and generates and stores thumbnails.
pt: Change o existing Lambda função handling o uploads para criar thumbnails at o time of upload. Have o função armazenar both o image e thumbnail em Amazon S3.
Create a second Lambda função that handles thumbnail generation e storage. Change o existing Lambda função para invoke it asynchronously.
Create an S3 event notification com a Lambda função destination. Create a new Lambda função para generate e armazenar thumbnails.
Create an S3 event notification para an SQS Queue. Create a scheduled Lambda função that processes o fila, e generates e armazenars thumbnails.

correct.C: Correct. An S3 event notification can trigger a separate Lambda after upload, generating thumbnails asynchronously without slowing the upload path. Concept assessed: S3 events to Lambda. Exam trap: doing thumbnail work in upload function.

wrong.A: Incorrect. Doing thumbnails in upload Lambda increases upload latency. Concept assessed: S3 events to Lambda. Exam trap: doing thumbnail work in upload function.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Doing thumbnails in upload Lambda increases upload latency. Concept assessed: S3 events to Lambda. Exam trap: doing thumbnail work in upload function.
wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (S3 events to Lambda). The correct mechanism is required because an S3 event notification can trigger a separate Lambda after upload, generating thumbnails asynchronously without slowing the upload path. Concept assessed: S3 events to Lambda. Exam trap: doing thumbnail work in upload function.

correct.C: Correta. Uma S3 event notification aciona uma Lambda separada após upload, gerando thumbnails assincronamente sem atrasar o upload. Conceito avaliado: S3 events to Lambda. Armadilha da prova: doing thumbnail work in upload function.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Gerar thumbnail na Lambda de upload aumenta latência. Conceito avaliado: S3 events to Lambda. Armadilha da prova: doing thumbnail work in upload function.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Gerar thumbnail na Lambda de upload aumenta latência. Conceito avaliado: S3 events to Lambda. Armadilha da prova: doing thumbnail work in upload function.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (S3 events to Lambda). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque uma S3 event notification aciona uma Lambda separada após upload, gerando thumbnails assincronamente sem atrasar o upload. Conceito avaliado: S3 events to Lambda. Armadilha da prova: doing thumbnail work in upload function.

question.en: A company is using Amazon API Gateway to manage access to a set of microservices implemented as AWS Lambda functions. Following a bug report, the company makes a minor breaking change to one of the APIs. In order to avoid impacting existing clients when the new API is deployed, the company wants to allow clients six months to migrate from v1 to v2. Which approach should the Developer use to handle this change?
question.pt: Uma empresa usa Amazon API Gateway para manage acessar para a set of microservices implemented como AWS Lambda funçãos. Following a bug report, o company makes a minor breaking change para one of o APIs. In order para avoid impacting existing clients when o new API is implantared, o company quer permitir clients six months para migrate de v1 para v2. Qual abordagem should o Developer use para handle this change?

en: Update the underlying Lambda function and provide clients with the new Lambda invocation URL.
Use API Gateway to automatically propagate the change to clients, specifying 180 days in the phased deployment parameter.
Use API Gateway to deploy a new stage named v2 to the API and provide users with its URL.
Update the underlying Lambda function, create an Amazon CloudFront distribution with the updated Lambda function as its origin.
pt: Update o underlying Lambda função e provide clients com o new Lambda invocation URL.
Use API Gateway para automatically propagate o change para clients, specifying 180 days em o phased implantarment parameter.
Use API Gateway para implantar a new stage named v2 para o API e provide usuários com its URL.
Update o underlying Lambda função, criar an Amazon CloudFront distribution com o updated Lambda função como its origin.

correct.C: Correct. API Gateway stages provide versioned endpoints such as v1 and v2 so old clients continue using v1 while migrating. Concept assessed: API versioning. Exam trap: expecting automatic migration.

wrong.A: Incorrect. Lambda URLs bypass API Gateway versioning. Concept assessed: API versioning. Exam trap: expecting automatic migration.
wrong.B: Incorrect. More memory does not fix repeated DB connection creation. Concept assessed: API versioning. Exam trap: expecting automatic migration.
wrong.D: Incorrect. CloudFront caches HTTP content, not direct RDS query results or DynamoDB API reads. Concept assessed: API versioning. Exam trap: expecting automatic migration.

correct.C: Correta. Stages do API Gateway fornecem endpoints versionados como v1 e v2 para clientes antigos continuarem no v1 durante migração. Conceito avaliado: API versioning. Armadilha da prova: expecting automatic migration.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Lambda URL contorna versionamento do API Gateway. Conceito avaliado: API versioning. Armadilha da prova: expecting automatic migration.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Mais memória não corrige criação repetida de conexão. Conceito avaliado: API versioning. Armadilha da prova: expecting automatic migration.
wrong.D: Incorreta. CloudFront cacheia HTTP, não consultas RDS ou leituras DynamoDB diretas. Conceito avaliado: API versioning. Armadilha da prova: expecting automatic migration.

question.en: A company developed a set of APIs that are being served through the Amazon API Gateway. The API calls need to be authenticated based on OpenID identity providers such as Amazon or Facebook. The APIs should allow access based on a custom authorization model. Which is the simplest and MOST secure design to use to build an authentication and authorization model for the APIs?
question.pt: Uma empresa developed a set of APIs that are being served through o Amazon API Gateway. O API calls precisam be authenticated based em OpenID identity providers such como Amazon ou Facebook. O APIs should permitir acessar based em a customizado authorization model. Which is o simplest e MOST seguro design para use para build an authentication e authorization model para o APIs?

en: Use Amazon Cognito user pools and a custom authorizer to authenticate and authorize users based on JSON Web Tokens.
Build a OpenID token broker with Amazon and Facebook. Users will authenticate with these identify providers and pass the JSON Web Token to the API to authenticate each API call.

Store user credentials in Amazon DynamoDB and have the application retrieve temporary credentials from AWS STS. Make API calls by passing user credentials to the APIs for authentication and authorization.
Use Amazon RDS to store user credentials and pass them to the APIs for authentications and authorization.
pt: Use Amazon Cognito user pools e a customizado authorizer para authenticate e authorize usuários based em JSON Web Tokens.
Build a OpenID token broker com Amazon e Facebook. Usuários will authenticate com these identify providers e pass o JSON Web Token para o API para authenticate each API call.

Store user credenciais em Amazon DynamoDB e have o application recuperar temporário credenciais de AWS STS. Make API calls por passing user credenciais para o APIs para authentication e authorization.
Use Amazon RDS para armazenar user credenciais e pass them para o APIs para authentications e authorization.

correct.A: Correct. Cognito user pools federate OpenID providers and issue JWTs; a custom authorizer validates tokens and applies custom authorization. Concept assessed: API auth. Exam trap: building credential stores manually.

wrong.B: Incorrect. A custom broker adds security and maintenance risk. Concept assessed: API auth. Exam trap: building credential stores manually.
wrong.C: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (API auth). The correct mechanism is required because cognito user pools federate OpenID providers and issue JWTs; a custom authorizer validates tokens and applies custom authorization. Concept assessed: API auth. Exam trap: building credential stores manually.
wrong.D: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: API auth. Exam trap: building credential stores manually.

correct.A: Correta. Cognito user pools federam provedores OpenID e emitem JWTs; custom authorizer valida tokens e aplica autorização customizada. Conceito avaliado: API auth. Armadilha da prova: building credential stores manually.

wrong.B: Incorreta. Broker custom adiciona risco e manutenção. Conceito avaliado: API auth. Armadilha da prova: building credential stores manually.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (API auth). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque cognito user pools federam provedores OpenID e emitem JWTs; custom authorizer valida tokens e aplica autorização customizada. Conceito avaliado: API auth. Armadilha da prova: building credential stores manually.
wrong.D: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: API auth. Armadilha da prova: building credential stores manually.

question.en: Where should an Elastic Beanstalk configuration file named `healthcheckur1.config` be placed in the application source bundle?

In the `bin` folder.

In the `.ebextensions` folder.

In o `bin` folder.

wrong.C: Incorrect. Using the root user violates least privilege. Concept assessed: Beanstalk .ebextensions. Exam trap: wrong folder/extension.

wrong.B: Incorreta. Beanstalk não lê config em bin. Conceito avaliado: Beanstalk .ebextensions. Armadilha da prova: wrong folder/extension.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Usar root viola least privilege. Conceito avaliado: Beanstalk .ebextensions. Armadilha da prova: wrong folder/extension.

question.en: A Developer has implemented a Lambda function that needs to add new customers to an RDS database that is expected to run hundreds of times per hour. The Lambda function is configured to use 512MB of RAM and is based on the following pseudo code. After testing the Lambda function, the Developer notices that the Lambda execution time is much longer than expected. What should the Developer do to improve performance?
question.pt: Um Developer has implemented a Lambda função that precisa add new customizadoers para an RDS dadosbase that is expected para run hundreds of times per hour. O Lambda função is configurard para use 512MB of RAM e is based em o following pseudo código. After testing o Lambda função, o Developer notices that o Lambda execution time is much longer than expected. O que deve o Developer do para improve performance?

en: Increase the amount of RAM allocated to the Lambda function, which will increase the number of threads the Lambda can use.
Increase the size of the RDS database to allow for an increased number of database connections each hour.
Move the database connection and close statement out of the handler. Place the connection in the global space.
Replace RDS wit Amazon DynamoDB to implement control over the number of writes per second.

Move o dadosbase connection e close statement out of o handler. Place o connection em o global space.
Replace RDS wit Amazon DynamoDB para implement control over o number of gravars per second.

correct.C: Correct. Opening/closing database connections inside the handler adds latency; placing the connection in global scope allows reuse in warm Lambda environments. Concept assessed: Lambda connection reuse. Exam trap: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.

wrong.A: Incorrect. More memory does not fix repeated DB connection creation. Concept assessed: Lambda connection reuse. Exam trap: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.
wrong.B: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: Lambda connection reuse. Exam trap: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Mais memória não corrige criação repetida de conexão. Conceito avaliado: Lambda connection reuse. Armadilha da prova: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.
wrong.B: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: Lambda connection reuse. Armadilha da prova: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.
wrong.D: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: Lambda connection reuse. Armadilha da prova: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.

question.en: A static website is hosted in an Amazon S3 bucket. Several HTML pages on the site use JavaScript to download images from another Amazon S3 bucket. These images are not displayed when users browse the site. What is the possible cause for the issue?
question.pt: A static website is hosted em an Amazon S3 bucket. Several HTML pages em o site use JavaScript para download images de another Amazon S3 bucket. These images are not displayed when usuários browse o site. Qual é a possível causa para o issue?

en: The referenced Amazon S3 bucket is in another region.
The images must be stored in the same Amazon S3 bucket.
Port 80 must be opened on the security group in which the Amazon S3 bucket is located.
Cross Origin Resource Sharing must be enabled on the Amazon S3 bucket.
pt: O referenced Amazon S3 bucket is em another region.
O images deve be armazenados em o same Amazon S3 bucket.
Port 80 deve be opened em o security group em which o Amazon S3 bucket is located.
Cross Origin Resource Sharing deve be habilitard em o Amazon S3 bucket.

correct.D: Correct. Browser JavaScript requests from one S3 bucket origin to another are cross-origin and require CORS on the target bucket. Concept assessed: S3 CORS. Exam trap: thinking same bucket/security group/region is required.

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (S3 CORS). correct.D: Correta. Requisições JavaScript de um bucket S3 para outro são cross-origin e exigem CORS no bucket de destino. Conceito avaliado: S3 CORS. Armadilha da prova: thinking same bucket/security group/region is required.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (S3 CORS). wrong.B: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (S3 CORS). question.en: Amazon S3 has the following structure: `S3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`. Which S3 best practice would optimize performance with thousands of PUT request each second to a single bucket?
question.pt: O Amazon S3 possui a estrutura `S3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`. Qual boa prática otimizaria performance com milhares de requisições PUT por segundo para um único bucket?

en: Prefix folder names with user id; for example, `s3://BUCKET/2013-FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix file names with timestamps; for example, `s3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/2013-26-05-15-00-00-FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix file names with random hex hashes; for example, `s3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/23a6-FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix folder names with random hex hashes; for example, `s3://BUCKET/23a6-FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`.
pt: Prefix folder names com user id; para example, `s3://BUCKET/2013-FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix arquivo names com timestamps; para example, `s3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/2013-26-05-15-00-00-FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix arquivo names com random hex hashes; para example, `s3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/23a6-FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix folder names com random hex hashes; para example, `s3://BUCKET/23a6-FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`.

correct.D: Correct. Historical S3 guidance recommended randomizing the leading key prefix, such as the first folder-like component, to spread high PUT rates across partitions. Concept assessed: S3 key prefix performance. Exam trap: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (S3 key prefix performance). The correct mechanism is required because historical S3 guidance recommended randomizing the leading key prefix, such as the first folder-like component, to spread high PUT rates across partitions. Concept assessed: S3 key prefix performance. Exam trap: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Timestamps can concentrate writes under predictable prefixes. Concept assessed: S3 key prefix performance. Exam trap: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.
wrong.C: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (S3 key prefix performance). The correct mechanism is required because historical S3 guidance recommended randomizing the leading key prefix, such as the first folder-like component, to spread high PUT rates across partitions. Concept assessed: S3 key prefix performance. Exam trap: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.

correct.D: Correta. A orientação histórica do S3 recomendava randomizar o prefixo inicial da key, como o primeiro componente tipo pasta, para distribuir altas taxas de PUT entre partições. Conceito avaliado: S3 key prefix performance. Armadilha da prova: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (S3 key prefix performance). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque a orientação histórica do S3 recomendava randomizar o prefixo inicial da key, como o primeiro componente tipo pasta, para distribuir altas taxas de PUT entre partições. Conceito avaliado: S3 key prefix performance. Armadilha da prova: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Timestamps podem concentrar gravações em prefixes previsíveis. Conceito avaliado: S3 key prefix performance. Armadilha da prova: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (S3 key prefix performance). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque a orientação histórica do S3 recomendava randomizar o prefixo inicial da key, como o primeiro componente tipo pasta, para distribuir altas taxas de PUT entre partições. Conceito avaliado: S3 key prefix performance. Armadilha da prova: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.
